meaning: n.犹太人集会,犹太教会堂,犹太教
examples: They had their synagogue and the five books of Moses.
In 1931, he was approached by the Rodef Shalom synagogue in Pittsburgh to stage the annual Kermess.
Real men occasionally attend church or synagogue.
In all the news, I never noticed the word synagogue or Jewish, associated with Madoff.
By hosting her, they intoned, the Great Neck Synagogue would be guilty of propagating hate speech.
